title: SDLC execution plan
description: The dependency graph + (optional) wave structure for a block-runnable spec, written to planning/<spec>/sdlc/execution-plan.json. Authored by /generate-tasks when it recommends a block runner (D22), so /sdlc-block's Analyze stage can LOAD the plan instead of re-deriving it on an Opus agent. The block VALIDATES this file on load — it must parse, match this schema, AND its task set (the keys of `tasks`) must match the current tasks.md `### N.` headings exactly (same count + numbering). A stale plan (tasks.md edited after authoring) is REJECTED and the block falls back to the Opus analyzer; loading a stale plan blindly would fan out the wrong waves. `waves` is optional — when absent the engine computes them deterministically from the graph.

blockId.description: The spec slug this plan belongs to (e.g. 'phase0-blockC').

additiveFiles.description: Shared files every touching task only APPENDS to (safe to union-merge), e.g. a barrel/index re-export, a registry/manifest, an auto-generated reference doc.

tasks.description: The dependency graph, keyed by task number (string key, e.g. "1", "2"). One entry per `### N.` task in tasks.md.

num.description: Task number (matches the key and the `### N.` heading).

dependsOn.description: Task numbers whose output this task consumes.

filesModified.description: Existing shared files this task edits.

evidence.description: Quote(s) from tasks.md establishing each dependsOn edge.

recommendBreakdown.description: true if this task is coarse enough to benefit from /breakdown (D10/D13 heuristic).

breakdownReason.description: One sentence naming the coarseness signal (only when recommendBreakdown).

waves.description: Optional. The wave structure (dependency-ordered batches). When absent the engine computes it deterministically from the graph. When present the engine uses it verbatim.
